paragraph: Single-family homes dominate the Teaneck market, with 147 sales over the past eight months at a median price of $666,900. This is a real seller's market. Homes are selling slightly above list at a 1.02 sale-to-list ratio, more than 60 percent of properties are closing above asking, and the median time on market sits at 21 days. You'll see everything from modest homes under $500,000 to significant properties pushing toward $3 million, which gives you range within a single category. Condos and townhouses, by contrast, are much thinner volume at 17 sales, but they're moving on similar terms. The median sits at $530,000, and they're closing above list with a median of 19 days on market. Co-ops round out the market at the entry point with a median of $190,000 across seven sales. They take longer to move, with a 42-day median on market, and buyers aren't pushing above asking the way they do in the single-family or condo tiers. If you're comparing tiers, the deciding factor isn't market heat. It's whether you want the space and grounds of a single-family, the low-maintenance lifestyle of a condo, or the lower price point of a co-op.
